original: “You wanna know more about me? Well, sure, I know some people don’t like talking about themselves, but I don’t mind. Have you ever been on Tarkus?” he asks. You tell him that you have been there. “Right, so you know that it’s a fucking shitty ball of sand and rusted shit floating in space, and that us raskvel are native to Tarkus. I was born there before the Rush, and when the first rushers landed there, I got aboard a rusher’s ship faster than an ausar knotting a bitch in heat,” he says, chuckling a little at his own dumb joke. “Why did you hate it there so much to risk it going, well, anywhere? You’re lucky you didn’t end up enslaved.” “Perhaps, but I was young, and real stupid. I mean, I was even denser back then than I am today. As for why I got out of there in such a hurry, I hated it there. It was a glorified scrapyard, and it was either sand, or rust, or some fucked up natural hazard. And even when the planet isn’t trying to kill you, you have to watch your ass for the gray goo, the broken sex bots trying to shove things in you, the other rasks, and the fucking sydians.” You can’t help but to chuckle a little as the boy bristles up in anger from just thinking about sydians. “Come on, they can’t be that bad, Sure they ruin shields, but they’re not the worst you see in the frontier.” “Ok yeah, maybe, but when your entire survival is based on recycling scraps and repairing broken down electronics, having some chitin-head come and eat your livelihood makes my blood boil,” he pauses and crosses his hands, “But I have to give the rust freaks some credit, without them, I probably wouldn’t have learned to maintain weapons. They’re not stupid, so when they have to choose between a rask with an overclocked laser pistol, or a rask with some old, barely functioning pistol, or even unarmed, who do you think they’re going to go for?” You don’t even need to respond, it’s obvious enough. “I always had a knack for maintaining weapons, and they gave me a good excuse to keep getting better. Eventually I learned how to even restore completely broken ones, and started gaining a bit of a reputation for knowing how to fix up guns, so I ended up selling and repairing weapons for other Raskvel, and anyone else who paid, really.” He pauses to think for a while, so you ask him what he’s up to now that he’s on Dhaal. “Well, I don’t have to dig in sand all day, so I can actually have some hobbies; I like listening to music, and doing some yoga — helps with being nice and flexible in the bedroom,” he chuckles softly, grinning a little. “I also tinker with guns in my free time, trying to improve on my craft. And, well, loads and loads of porn. Hell, I tend to read porn mags when there aren’t any customers around,” he says, showing the magazine he was reading to you, a cross-section filled with tits and asses. “Don’t your bosses get mad that you’re watching porn at work?” “I am my boss, I own this whole building, so no, my bosses won’t get mad. I do answer to Paragon, but as long as I keep the credits rolling and guns changing hands, they don’t care what I do in between arming rushers. But that’s enough of me, wouldn’t want to spend the entire day talking about myself.

original: “So, how exactly did you end up on Dhaal? I remember you saying you hitched a ride from some rushers, but there’s gotta be more to it than that.” “Well, when I heard that there were ships coming from off world, I got to the ship port as fast as I could. I was basically standing around with a sign saying take me anywhere but here. Eventually a captain of a rusher crew approached me, and after talking to her for a bit, and mentioning that I can maintain their weapons, she agreed to take me off world. I’m not sure if it was exactly legal, but I’m not complaining,” he says with a little grin. “The first night aboard the ship, I learned that you rushers can have libidos to outmatch even the thirstiest rask. Hell, they’re probably part of the reason why I’m such a horny little bastard. What the captain hadn’t mentioned was that her crew consisted of nothing but buff herms, so while my official duty aboard the ship was armorer, I spent more time with girl cock in my ass than fixing guns. I mean, I didn’t dislike it, but there’s only so much I can get of my prostate being demolished before I get bored of it.” “So, what happened to the crew?” “We landed on Dhaal, and that’s the last I saw of them. Don’t know why, maybe they became milk slaves to the zaika, or maybe they were fucking around for long enough that I left the ship. Took a bunch of guns with me as well, they haven’t come asking for ‘em, so...” he shrugs. “But I do hope they’re safe, I hold no ill will towards them, even if my ass did for a week after seeing them for the last time.” “Now that you mention it, Dhaal isn’t the safest place, not that Tarkus was particularly safe either. But, how are you liking Dhaal so far?” “Well, it has some good, and some bad. As an upside, I don’t live in a scrapyard, and I actually have a bed, and a rather nice one at that. I have all the credits I could ever want, the women, the drugs. Anything I could want, I can get, and sure, it’s dangerous, but I live in the back of my shop, and this place is a god-damn bunker, so I’m as safe as you can be on Dhaal without being a zaibatsu. Not that Dhaal’s perfect, far from that. If I didn’t work for Paragon, this shop would be illegal, and I have no interest in being made into one of their security slaves. But since I work for them, a lot of the worst of their problems don’t affect me. Hell, if I were to get robbed, they’d actually might care since I provide them with money and arms I get from rushers. It’s a weird, protection racket, partnership, and supplier mix. I pay them, but they provide me with stock.” “So, what’s working for Paragon like?” “Honestly, I don’t even really notice it most of the time. But that’s probably because I’m closer to a contractor for them. They want their share bi-weekly, and it’s quite a hefty cut as well, but they provide me with the stock so, I can’t complain. But, if things get bad, they certainly don’t help, they often make things worse, don’t care what happened, payments must be made on the day, no excuses. I’ve been late only once on the payment, and that was before I had upgraded the security and someone tried to rob me. I didn’t have the machine guns in the ceiling yet, had to resort to taking them out myself. Thankfully, I’m always packing heat,” he says with a grin, pointing out his fingers like that of a pistol, revealing a concealed barrel inside his finger. “It’s not much, but when people don’t expect it, it makes all the difference.” He shakes his head and chuckles a little, “Sorry, I got caught talking about myself again. After the attempted robbery I had to install the new security, so the place was closed for a while, meaning no customers, which naturally meant no credits coming in. They slapped a fine on me for that, which I managed to thankfully pay off, so they didn’t manage to bullshit me into debt slavery. So yeah, Dhaal’s got a fuck ton of problems, but I still can’t bring myself to hate it like I do the tetanus central called Tarkus.
context: <<POS:02419280>> textify(['<i>“So, how exactly did you end up on Dhaal? I rem'...])

context: <> addButton(e++,"Weapons",(function(){Ic(),output(textify(hd||(hd=(0,Bt.Z)(["“Can you tell me a bit more about the guns on sale?”\n\n\t\t“Sure, the guns we have in stock are a variety of elemental-based arms, using either corrosive, thermal, or cryotic. original: “Can you tell me a bit more about the guns on sale?” “Sure, the guns we have in stock are a variety of elemental-based arms, using either corrosive, thermal, or cryotic. Ballistic weapons tend to get somewhat shunned here —much to my dismay— since it’s easier to paint out scorch marks than to fix golf ball sized holes. Also, since the technology developed on Dhaal was originally designed for mining on both sides of the planet, the technology is more thermally focused to melt the ice on the frozen side, and the opposite on the other side.” “So weapons on Dhaal are refurbished mining equipment?” “Well, not quite, they used to be, but the tech has advanced enough that they’re a separate branch now. Paragon’s got their own R&D lab where they come up with these puppies. Of course, I check the quality of each piece I sell; I won’t be caught dead selling a broken weapon. Also fine-tuning them, polishing lenses, cleaning coils, that sort of thing.” “Huh, you really take pride in your craft, huh?” “Yeah, I even got a couple of pieces I made myself in the back. Designed them back on Tarkus. Back then they were just a pipe dream, but here I can actually get the parts needed. Spend most of my time tweaking with them, but they still need a lot more work before they’re ready for test firing. Don’t know what I’ll do with them once I’m happy with them. Don’t think I’ll sell, would rather have someone who knows how to put ‘em to good use.” You can’t help but to laugh a little, “You really love your guns, huh.” He grins, rubbing the back of his neck. “Well, yeah, I guess you could say that. I do like guns, sleek and elegant, and so much power,” he raises one finger up, looking at you seriously, “but they also demand respect. They’re tools like any other, but they’re among the deadliest tools there are. You treat them wrong, you’re going to pay for it.” He clenches his robotic hand before splaying out his fingers. “Learned that the hard way.” You can’t help but to ask what happened. “I’ll spare you the details, but I was overcharging a loaded weapon, and it blew up on me,” he says, making you reflexively grimace. “But I promise that no weapon I sell you is going to blow up on you, you have my word on it. So, with that little talk out of the way, looking to buy?”
context: <<POS:02423545>> textify(['<i>“Can you tell me a bit more about the guns on s'...])

original: You lean closer against the bulletproof glass, “Say, since you own the shop and all, can you give me a discount?” He crosses his arms and raises an eyebrow, the corners of his lips curling upwards ever so slightly. “Oh, and why should I?” Leaning closet against the glass, you lick your lips, speaking in your best seductive tone. “Well, I can make it worth your while.” His grin grows wider, and there’s a mischievous glint in his eye. “Oh, what is that then,” he asks, feigning ignorance. “I’m sure a smart guy like you can figure out what I have in mind,” you whisper huskily, close enough to fog up the protective barrier. He bursts out laughing. “I’ve never had someone with the spine to come into my shop, and suggest fucking for a discount, but you know what, sure, I’ll give you a one-time discount if you’re willing to be a pet for twenty-four hours. However, I have a better deal for you. Let’s just say that if you were to somehow come across some weapons from either Bright Water or Utopian Sky, well, in theory I’d take them off of your hands, and give you a lifetime discount and something on top of that.” “Well, that worked better than I expected,” you admit, grinning a little.

original: You tell him that you’d like to take him up on his earlier offer to have some fun with him. “Oh, is that so? Dread starts growing at the bottom of your stomach. You have no idea where you are, or how to get back to your ship. “Lost are we, my darling?” you hear a voice echo, elegant and soft. You don’t know if it’s in your head or coming from somewhere. It feels like it’s both. You turn around, looking around in the featureless void, trying to locate the source of the sound. The air in front of you distorts and flickers, before a figure steps out of the thin air. A zaika appears in front of you. [flags.MET_CORELLAH|It’s Corellah Dova, but it doesn’t make sense, how could she be here with you?|You don’t know who she is, but simply how she’s dressed makes you think she’s someone important. But how could someone like her be here with you.] “You seem to be confused, my darling,” she says casually as she walks closer to you.
